memoryArtifacts.headline: BIO
memoryArtifacts.description:
BIO:  Vicomte d'Avranches.  Witnessed a charter, 1064, 1066; perhaps at the Battle of Hastings; if so he was a companion of William the Conqueror.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/NORMAN%20NOB …

zh.Avranches, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Fran: 法國諾門迪曼其阿福朗徹斯
zh.Avranches, Manche, Normandy, France: 法國諾門迪曼其阿福朗徹斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches於1025年10月19日出生在法國諾門迪曼其阿福朗徹斯時，他的父親Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches36歲，母親Judith Montanolier30歲。他和Emma de Conteville至少有1個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1082年之後在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Avranches, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Fran: 法国諾門迪曼其阿福朗徹斯
zh-hans.Avranches, Manche, Normandy, France: 法国諾門迪曼其阿福朗徹斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches于1025年10月19日出生在法国諾門迪曼其阿福朗徹斯时，他的父亲Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches36岁，母亲Judith Montanolier30岁。他和Emma de Conteville至少有1个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1082年之后在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Goz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Veneto): from a short form of a personal name such as Giacomozzo, a derivative of Giacomo . Alternatively, it may be a nickname from the dialect word moz ‘cut off’, perhaps referring to some physical characteristic, perhaps a result of an accident.

Hispanic (El Salvador): altered form of Galician and Portuguese Mós, topographic name from the plural of mó ‘millstone’ or a habitational name from one of several places called Mós in Galicia (Spain) and Portugal.

variants.name: Giacomo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenico, Gino, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Giacomo, from Latin Jacobus via Late Latin Jacomus (see James and Jacob ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches was born on 19 October 1025, in Avranches, Manche, Normandy, France, his father, Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches, was 36 and his mother, Judith Montanolier, was 30. He had at least 1 son and 4 daughters with Emma de Conteville. He died after 1082, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches est né le 19 octobre 1025, à Avranches, Manche, Normandie, France, son père, Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Judith Montanolier, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles avec Emma de Conteville. Il est décédé après 1082, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches am 19. Oktober 1025 in Avranches, Manche, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches 36 und seine Mutter Judith Montanolier 30. Er hatte mit Emma de Conteville mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb nach 1082 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches è nato il il 19 ottobre 1025 nel Avranches, Manche, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Judith Montanolier, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie con Emma de Conteville. Morì dopo il 1082, nel sua cittadina.

ja.Avranches, Manche, Normandy, France: フランスノーマンディマンチェアvランチェス
ja.lifeSummary: Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranchesが1025年10月19日にフランスノーマンディマンチェアvランチェスで生まれた時，父Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranchesは36歳，母Judith Montanolierは30歳であった。彼はEmma de Contevilleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1082年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches(이)가 1025년 10월 19일 프랑스 노먼디 먼체 아브랭체스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith Montanolier(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Emma de Conteville(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1082년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches nasceu em 19 de outubro de 1025, em Avranches, Manche, Normandie, França, seu pai, Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Judith Montanolier, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas com Emma de Conteville. Ele faleceu após 1082, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches родился 19 октября 1025 г. в местности авранчесь, манч, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith Montanolier, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери от Emma de Conteville. Он умер после 1082 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard le Goz Viscount of Avranches nació el 19 de octubre de 1025, en Avranches, Manche, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Thurstan le Goz Viscomte d'Avranches, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Judith Montanolier, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas con Emma de Conteville. Murió después de 1082, en su pueblo.
